description: Complete the Rust port of Talon (Obsidian vault search engine) from where Phase 4 ended. Phases 0-4 (workspace, store, search algorithms, indexer/sync, embed pipeline, sqlite-vec) are landed with 206 passing tests. Remaining work: query handlers (search/read/related/meta/changes/lint/status), stateless MCP server, TS wrapper with per-platform binaries, parity tests, and final polish. Reference TS at /tmp/talon-scaffold-imports/ultra/edge/src/services/talon/. Architectural decision LOCKED per docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-talon-extraction-design.md §2/§4: Talon is stateless and manual — NO file watcher, NO scheduler, NO clock-driven background work in Rust. The ultraclaw TS watcher remains the caller and spawns `talon sync`. `--mcp` is a stateless stdio request/response server, not a daemon. Spec also locks: §5 binary surface lists no `talon embed` command (embed runs inside `talon sync`, `--fast` skips it); Decision 8 locks the response envelope as `{action, version, ok, data, meta}` for success and `{action, version, ok: false, error}` for failures; Decision 12 puts the agent skill at `skill/SKILL.md` at repo root (not docs/); Decision 14 names the npm package `@seanmozeik/talon` with platform subpackages `@seanmozeik/talon-<platform>-<arch>`. Standing rules: never silence clippy lints with #[allow] (real fixes only); use wiremock for sidecar HTTP tests; real rusqlite Connection for DB tests (no mocks); pin deps to full semver; project conventions are eyre/color-eyre, time (not chrono), fs-err (not std::fs), serde_yaml_ng, blocking rusqlite + reqwest::blocking + spawn_blocking.

userStories.title: Add LLM expansion HTTP client
userStories.description: As Talon, I want a blocking OpenAI-compatible chat client so the hybrid search pipeline can request query expansion variants from the local LLM.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/expansion/{mod,client,types,error}.rs
Public surface: ExpansionClient::new(base_url, model) and expand(query: &str, n_variants: u8) -> Result<Vec<String>, ExpansionError>
POSTs /chat/completions with system prompt ported from search/hybrid-expand.ts
Returns empty Vec on malformed JSON (never panics); HTTP errors map to ExpansionError
PII redaction reuses inference::redact
Wiremock tests: happy path, malformed-JSON fallback, HTTP 5xx
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/expansion/{mod,client,types,error}.rs. ExpansionClient::new(base_url, model) + expand(query, n_variants) -> Result<Vec<String>, ExpansionError>. Posts /chat/completions with system prompt from local-llm.ts. Malformed JSON returns Ok(vec![]). HTTP errors map to ExpansionError::Http. PII redaction reuses inference::redact. Wiremock tests cover happy path, malformed-JSON fallback, and HTTP 5xx. 212 tests pass.

userStories.title: Add persistent llm_cache with TTL

acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/expansion/cache.rs
Public surface: LlmCache::new(conn), get(key), put(key, value, ttl_ms), purge_expired()
Uses existing llm_cache table from migrations (key, value, expires_at_ms columns)
Tests: round-trip, expiry past TTL returns None, purge_expired removes stale rows
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/expansion/cache.rs: LlmCache<'conn> with new(conn), get(key), put(key, value, ttl_ms), purge_expired(). Updated llm_cache migration to (key, value, expires_at_ms) schema. Exported from expansion::mod and lib.rs. 5 tests (round-trip, missing-key, expired-entry, overwrite, purge). 217 tests pass.

userStories.title: Compose hybrid-single retrieval
userStories.description: As Talon, I want a single-query retrieval function that runs bm25 + fuzzy_title + vector in parallel and returns the three result lists for downstream RRF fusion.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/search/hybrid_single.rs
Public surface: run_hybrid_single(conn, query, embedding: Option<&[f32]>, limit) -> HybridSingleResult { bm25, fuzzy_title_parts: TitleSearchParts, vector }
Composes existing search::bm25::search_bm25, search::fuzzy_title::search_title_parts, search::vector::search_vector — no new algorithm code
Tests against seeded fixture: each retriever's contribution is isolated and complete
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/search/hybrid_single.rs. HybridSingleResult { bm25, fuzzy_title_parts: TitleSearchParts, vector }. run_hybrid_single(conn, query, embedding: Option<&[f32]>, limit) composes search_bm25, search_title_parts, search_vector. embedding=None skips vector call and returns empty bucket. Re-exported HybridSingleResult and run_hybrid_single from search::mod. 5 tests: bm25/fuzzy-title/vector bucket isolation, None-embedding guard, disjoint-note independence. 222 tests pass.

userStories.title: Build cross-encoder rerank pipeline
userStories.description: As Talon, I want a reranker that calls the sidecar /rerank endpoint and blends the cross-encoder scores into the hybrid scores using rank-tier weights.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/search/rerank_pipeline.rs
Public surface: rerank_candidates(inference, query, candidates: Vec<RawSearchResult>, top_k: u32) -> Vec<RawSearchResult>
Calls inference.rerank(query, texts, return_text=false), then composes existing fuse::blend_rerank_candidates
Honors RERANK_TOP_K from search/constants
Wiremock test for /rerank; blend math matches TS expectations within 1e-4
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/search/rerank_pipeline.rs. rerank_candidates(inference, query, candidates, top_k) calls inference.rerank with title+"\n\n"+snippet payload, composes fuse::blend_rerank_candidates. top_k caps candidates sent to reranker. Graceful degradation on HTTP error returns original candidates with hybrid scores unchanged. Added RERANK_TOP_K=40 to search/constants. 5 wiremock tests: happy path, blend math within 1e-4, HTTP 5xx fallback, empty-candidates early exit, top_k truncation. 227 tests pass.

userStories.title: Undo `talon embed` subcommand and fold the embed pass into `talon sync`
userStories.description: As a Talon operator, I want `talon sync` to embed pending chunks as part of the same call (per spec §5 line 188-204) so the binary surface matches the spec — `--fast` on sync skips the embed pass; there is no separate `talon embed` command. The previously-committed `talon embed` command violated the spec and must be removed.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete the `"embed" => emit_embed(...)` arm from talon-cli/src/command.rs
Delete emit_embed function entirely (do NOT leave it as a hidden alias)
Remove EmbedInput / EmbedResponse from TalonInput / TalonResponse in talon-core/src/tool.rs (the embed action is not in the spec's MCP tool surface — Decision 10)

Extend run_sync(conn, vault_root, lock_path, indexer_config, embed_config: Option<EmbedPassOptions>, inference: Option<&InferenceClient>) — when embed_config is Some AND inference is Some AND not --fast, run the embed pass after reconcile_deletions

Extend SyncResponse with embed-pass fields: { embedded: u32, embed_failed: u32, dimension_mismatch: bool, embed_remediation: Option<String>, embed_diagnostics: Vec<String> } — fold the existing remediation hint here
Update emit_sync in command.rs: when --fast is absent, build InferenceClient + EmbedPassOptions from config and pass through; --fast skips inference build entirely (no useless network setup)
Update output.rs::emit_sync_human to print the embed sub-line (e.g. `Embedded N notes (M failed)` and the remediation hint when set)
Existing wiremock embed tests in talon-core/src/embed/runner.rs continue to pass — they test run_embed_pass directly and don't depend on the CLI command
Add an integration test crates/talon-core/tests/sync_embed.rs: temp vault → talon sync (with mocked sidecar) → vec_chunks populated; talon sync --fast → vec_chunks empty
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented: removed talon embed CLI subcommand and emit_embed handler. Removed EmbedInput/EmbedResponse from TalonInput/TalonResponse. Extended run_sync() to accept Option<EmbedPassOptions> + Option<&InferenceClient> — runs embed pass after reconciliation when both are Some. Extended SyncResponse with embedded/embed_failed/dimension_mismatch/embed_remediation/embed_diagnostics fields. Updated emit_sync to build InferenceClient from config when --fast absent. Updated emit_sync_human to print embed sub-line. Fixed vec_chunks INSERT in persist.rs (chunk_id not rowid for sqlite-vec virtual tables). Added 3 integration tests in crates/talon-core/tests/sync_embed.rs. 232 tests pass.

userStories.title: Build hybrid-pipeline orchestrator
userStories.description: As Talon, I want the full hybrid search pipeline (probe, optional expansion, multi-variant retrieval, fuse, rerank, filter) wired together so the search command can call one function.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/search/hybrid_pipeline.rs
Public surface: run_hybrid_pipeline(conn, inference, expansion, query, options) -> Vec<RawSearchResult>
Strong-signal short-circuit via fuse::estimate_strong_signal — skips expansion+rerank when probe is decisive
Calls US-001 expansion + US-003 hybrid-single per variant + US-004 rerank
Wiremock end-to-end test: full pipeline against seeded fixture vault with mocked sidecar (chat + embed + rerank)
Strong-signal path verified: high-confidence query skips expansion call
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Module talon-core/src/search/hybrid_pipeline.rs already implemented with 5 wiremock tests. Fixed clippy violations blocking verification: redundant clones in variant query assembly, uninlined format args in sync_embed.rs tests, #[ignore] without reason in bm25 diagnostic test. 232 tests pass.

userStories.title: Wrap TalonResponse in the spec-locked {action, version, ok, data, meta} envelope
userStories.description: As an MCP / agent client, I want every Talon response to use the envelope locked by Decision 8 — `{action, version, ok: true, data, meta}` for success, `{action, version, ok: false, error}` for failure — so downstream contracts are stable. The current `#[serde(tag = "action")]` flattens action-specific fields onto the top level with no `ok`, no `version`, no `meta`. The doc comment in tool.rs already declares this contract; the implementation must be brought in line.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace TalonResponse enum with a TalonEnvelope struct: { action: String (kebab-case), version: String (env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")), ok: bool, data: Option<TalonResponseData>, meta: Option<ResponseMeta>, error: Option<ErrorEnvelope> }

TalonResponseData becomes the action-discriminated payload (the existing variants: Search, Read, Sync, Status, Related, Meta, Changes, Lint), serialized via #[serde(untagged)] OR a sibling tag — whichever yields the spec's nested `data: { ... }` shape
Helper constructors: TalonEnvelope::ok(action, data, meta) and TalonEnvelope::err(action, error)
All CLI command handlers in talon-cli/src/command.rs construct envelopes via the helpers — no raw struct literals
All emit_*_human formatters in talon-cli/src/output.rs unwrap the envelope's data field before rendering
JSON output (--json / --agent) emits the full envelope per spec

Tests: serde round-trip for one variant of each shape; assert top-level keys are exactly action/version/ok/data/meta on success and action/version/ok/error on failure; existing CLI integration tests updated to read through envelope
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented TalonEnvelope struct replacing TalonResponse enum. Success shape: {action, version, ok: true, data, meta}. Error shape: {action, version, ok: false, error}. TalonResponseData tagged enum wraps all 8 response variants. Helper constructors TalonEnvelope::ok() and TalonEnvelope::err(). CLI command.rs uses envelope helpers everywhere. output.rs formatters unwrap data field. camelCase on ResponseMeta + ErrorEnvelope. ErrorCode added Display. 15 round-trip serde tests. 250 tests pass.

userStories.title: Wire real search command
userStories.description: As a Talon CLI user, I want `talon search` to return real ranked results across hybrid/semantic/fulltext/title modes with scope-priority post-multiplication, plus --where and --since post-filtering.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace talon-cli/src/command.rs::emit_search_stub with real handler calling US-005
Modes: hybrid (default), semantic (vector only), fulltext (bm25 only), title (fuzzy_title only)
Query embedding via blocking inference.embed for semantic/hybrid modes
--where <KEY OP VALUE> (repeatable) post-filters results by frontmatter (reuses WhereClause/WhereOperator from tool.rs and the meta handler's filter primitive — coordinate with US-009)
--since <TS> post-filters results to notes whose mtime >= parsed timestamp (reuses change_tracking::parse_since)
Post-rerank: multiply each result.score by ScopePriority::multiplier(), then re-sort
--fast flag skips expansion + skips rerank (per spec §5 line 225)
SearchResponse::empty_scaffold call sites removed
Integration test: end-to-end on temp vault (run_sync + search) with wiremock sidecar, including --where and --since cases
Wraps result in the US-005b envelope
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Wired real search handler calling run_search() in talon-core/src/query/search.rs. Supports hybrid/semantic/fulltext/title modes. --where frontmatter filters and --since timestamp filters applied post-retrieval. ScopePriority::multiplier() multiplied post-rerank and results re-sorted. --fast skips expansion and rerank. Removed SearchResponse::empty_scaffold (no call sites remain). Changed SearchResult.score/raw_score from f32 to f64 to fix cast lints. Fixed 22 clippy violations. 6-test integration suite in tests/search_integration.rs. 256 tests pass.

userStories.title: Build read handler

Supports --from-line / --max-lines slicing

Returns headings tree extracted from body

Tests: read fixture note with frontmatter, line range, raw mode, missing file → InvalidInput

userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/query/read.rs: run_read(conn, vault_root, &ReadInput) -> ReadResponse. Queries notes table for vault_path + active=1. Strips frontmatter via parse_frontmatter unless raw=true. Applies 1-based from_line/max_lines slicing. Hydrates links (outgoing), backlinks, tags, aliases from relational tables. Missing notes return found: false; empty path returns empty results list. Replaced emit_read_stub in talon-cli/command.rs. Updated emit_read_human to render title/path/tags/links/body. Exported run_read from talon-core lib.rs. 5 tests: frontmatter strip, raw mode, line range, missing note, links+backlinks. 261 tests pass.

Reuses existing links::compute_backlinks and resolve_wiki_links
Scope filter applied during traversal

userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/query/related.rs: find_related(conn, &RelatedInput) -> RelatedResponse. BFS traversal with cycle detection (visited HashSet), supports Outgoing/Backlinks/Both direction, depth clamped to RELATED_MAX_DEPTH=3. Scope filter via passes_scope_filter applied per neighbor. Title hydrated from notes table. Fixed clippy manual_let_else lint. Replaced emit_related_stub in talon-cli/src/command.rs with real emit_related handler opening the index DB. Exported from query::mod and lib.rs. 5 tests: outgoing depth-1, outgoing depth-2 transitive, backlinks, both directions, orphan. 266 tests pass.

userStories.title: Build meta (frontmatter query) handler

--where operators: =, !=, <, <=, >, >=, contains, exists (existing WhereClause/WhereOperator types)
--select projects requested frontmatter fields onto each entry
--tag-counts emits SELECT tag, COUNT(*) FROM note_tags GROUP BY tag
--sources resolves via ReverseSourceIndex
--since applies time-window filter
Replaces talon-cli/src/command.rs::emit_meta_stub
Expose the WhereClause matcher as a reusable primitive (e.g. apply_where_clauses(rows, clauses) -> Vec<row>) so US-006 search --where can share the implementation rather than reimplement it
Tests: each operator on fixture vault, tag aggregation, source lookup
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/query/meta.rs: query_meta(conn, &MetaInput) -> MetaResponse. Supports --where (8 operators via shared query/where_filter.rs primitives), --since timestamp filter, --select field projection from notes.frontmatter JSON, --tag-counts aggregation via note_tags, and --sources reverse-reference lookup via note_frontmatter_fields. CLI gains --select/--tag-counts/--sources flags via MetaArgs sub-struct. emit_meta replaces emit_meta_stub in command.rs. 8 unit tests. 274 tests pass.

userStories.title: Build changes handler
userStories.description: As an agent, I want `talon changes --since <timestamp>` to return the added/modified/deleted notes from the event_log.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/query/changes.rs with get_changes(conn, since: Option<i64>) -> ChangesResponse
Returns { added, modified, deleted } populated from event_log table
--since parsing reuses change_tracking::parse_since
Replaces the bail!("changes is scaffolded but not implemented yet") in command.rs
Tests: simulate add → modify → delete sequence via direct DB writes; verify classification
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/query/changes.rs: query_changes(conn, &ChangesInput) -> ChangesResponse. Reads all event_log rows, parses RFC3339 timestamps to ms via rfc3339_to_ms helper, and classifies: added (first 'index' event in window), modified ('index' in window with prior 'index' before window), deleted ('delete' event in window). Reuses change_tracking::parse_since. Scope filter applied per-entry; results sorted by timestamp; limit respected. Replaced bail! stub in command.rs with emit_changes handler. Exported query_changes from query::mod and lib.rs; added ChangesInput to lib.rs pub use. 5 unit tests. 279 tests pass.

userStories.title: Build lint handler
userStories.description: As an agent, I want `talon lint <check>` to surface graph health issues: orphans, broken-links, dangling-refs, unreferenced.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/query/lint.rs with run_lint(conn, check: LintCheck) -> LintResponse
Orphans: notes with zero incoming links
BrokenLinks: link target file does not exist
DanglingRefs: frontmatter reference to nonexistent path
Unreferenced: zero incoming AND zero outgoing
Replaces talon-cli/src/command.rs::emit_lint_stub
Tests: each check on fixture vault (Graph/Grandchild=orphan, Lifecycle/Doomed→nonexistent=broken)
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/query/lint.rs: query_lint(conn, &LintInput) -> LintResponse. Four SQL-backed checks: orphans (no incoming links via NOT IN subquery on links.to_path), broken-links (links.to_path not in active notes), dangling-refs (note_frontmatter_fields values ending in .md not in active notes), unreferenced (neither incoming nor outgoing links). Scope filter applied to all checks. Replaced emit_lint_stub in talon-cli/src/command.rs with real emit_lint handler opening the index DB. Exported query_lint from query/mod.rs and lib.rs. 5 unit tests. 284 tests pass.

userStories.title: Replace status stub with real index counts
userStories.description: As an operator, I want `talon status` to report real index health: active note count, chunk count, failed-embedding count, vector dimensions, scope report.
acceptanceCriteria: Replaces StatusResponse::scaffold and command.rs::emit_status_stub
Real counts via SQL: COUNT(*) FROM notes WHERE active=1, COUNT(*) FROM chunks, COUNT(*) FROM chunks WHERE embedding_status='failed'
Vector dimensions via vec_ext::get_vec_chunks_dimensions
Scope report: configured scopes, default set, unscoped count
State: Ready when vault reachable + index exists; ConfigError when talon init not run
Tests: status against seeded vault, status with no DB returns ConfigError
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/query/status.rs: query_status(conn, config) -> StatusResponse. SQL counts: active notes (WHERE active=1), total chunks, failed chunks (embedding_status='failed'). Vector dimensions via vec_ext::get_vec_chunks_dimensions. Scope report: total_scopes from config.scopes.len(), default_scopes from config.default_scope_names(), unscoped_count via glob matching against active note paths. emit_status in talon-cli/src/command.rs handles config/DB load failures gracefully (returns StatusState::ConfigError instead of propagating errors). Removed StatusResponse::scaffold(); added ContainerPath::root() infallible constructor; exported ScopeReport and query_status from lib.rs. 5 unit tests. 289 tests pass.

userStories.title: Port fixture vault and add integration test suite
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the 21-note fixture vault from the TS reference ported into a real on-disk fixture so integration tests exercise the full query layer end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Port 21 notes from /tmp/talon-scaffold-imports/.../tests/fixtures/talon/fixture-vault.ts to crates/talon-core/tests/fixtures/vault/ (Atlas/*, Graph/*, Search/*, Filters/*, Lifecycle/*)
New integration test crates/talon-core/tests/fixture_vault.rs
Runs run_sync (which now also embeds per US-004b) with mocked sidecar, then exercises: search hybrid, search fulltext, related, meta-where, lint orphans, status
Asserts paths exact-match TS expectations from tests/services/talon/fixture-vault.integration.test.ts; scores within 1e-3
Suite green across 5 consecutive runs with no flake
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Ported 21 notes to crates/talon-core/tests/fixtures/vault/ (Atlas/*, Graph/*, Search/*, Filters/*, Lifecycle/*). Added fixture_vault.rs with 9 integration tests: sync counts all 21 notes, fulltext search (orchard/banana), title alias search (Cafe del Sol), hybrid search with mocked expansion+rerank, related graph traversal (Hub depth=2 finds Child/Side/Inbound/Grandchild), meta --where status=archived returns only Filters/Frontmatter.md, lint orphans excludes linked notes, status reports 21 active notes + 5-dim vectors. EmbedChunkedResponder dynamically returns N vectors per chunk to satisfy persist_multi_chunk. 298 tests pass.

userStories.title: Redesign chunker on text-splitter + tokenx-rs: strip frontmatter from embedding/BM25, semantic markdown chunking with token-aware sizing
userStories.description: As Talon, I want chunking and indexing to match the canonical Obsidian-flavored-markdown contract — frontmatter is filter-only (via the existing note_frontmatter_fields / note_tags / note_aliases / notes.title surfaces), never participates in embeddings or BM25 body search; chunking is built on `text-splitter` (benbrandt's MarkdownSplitter — semantic markdown chunker with a multi-level fallback ladder: section → sentence → word → grapheme) with `tokenx-rs` as the pluggable length function (heuristic ~96% token-accuracy, no vocabulary files, no model binding); chunk size and overlap are user-configurable in talon.toml; Obsidian comments (%%...%%) are stripped pre-chunk; wikilink resolution still sees raw frontmatter (re-attach pattern from obsidian-hybrid-search). Reuses crate::frontmatter::parse_frontmatter (already returns body and frontmatter_raw). Standing rule: never silence clippy lints with #[allow] — real fixes only.
acceptanceCriteria: New deps pinned to full semver: text-splitter, tokenx-rs (project convention is full pinning)
talon-core/src/chunker.rs rewritten: chunk_markdown receives body-only text (frontmatter stripped at indexer/wiring.rs:61 boundary by passing parsed.body instead of content)
Chunker built on text_splitter::MarkdownSplitter with a tokenx-rs-backed length function; configured via .with_chunk_size(chunk_tokens) and .with_chunk_overlap(chunk_overlap)
Obsidian comments stripped pre-chunk: inline %%...%% and block %%\n...\n%% removed from cleaned_body before handing to MarkdownSplitter
Block IDs (^block-id) preserved inline in chunk text; never the sole content of a chunk (skip-pattern post-filter handles this)
Skip-patterns drop trivial chunks post-split: heading-only line, separator-only, single wikilink line, single image-embed line, content below CHUNK_MIN_TOKENS
embedding_text prefix (Title:/Path:/Headings:) unchanged — applied per chunk after splitting

Wikilink resolution continues to see raw frontmatter: pass parsed.frontmatter_raw + "\n" + parsed.body to resolve_wiki_links (matches obsidian-hybrid-search src/indexer.ts:132 pattern; resolves frontmatter values like `related: "[[Other Note]]"`)

User-configurable chunking knobs in talon.toml [indexer]: chunk_tokens (default 512), chunk_overlap (default 64), chunk_min_tokens (default 16); validated at config load time (chunk_overlap < chunk_tokens; chunk_min_tokens >= 0)
Constants in talon-core/src/search/constants.rs (or a new chunker module): EMBED_CHUNK_TOKENS_DEFAULT=512, EMBED_CHUNK_OVERLAP_DEFAULT=64, CHUNK_MIN_TOKENS_DEFAULT=16

No new schema and no other new dependencies: frontmatter is reachable only via existing channels (notes.frontmatter JSON, note_frontmatter_fields, note_tags, note_aliases, notes.title); reuses crate::frontmatter::parse_frontmatter

Existing chunker unit tests in chunker.rs (test_chunk_markdown_*) are updated, removed, and rewritten as needed — chunk shapes, line/char ranges, token counts, and chunk counts will all change with the new design; preserving old behavior is NOT a goal

Parser fidelity check: assert FrontmatterExtract.body is byte-faithful for a fixture file (== raw file with the leading ---
...
---
 block removed; trailing newline behavior pinned), so chunker char/line offsets stay consistent with what the user sees on disk

Existing chunk hashes invalidated by the redesign; next talon sync re-chunks and re-embeds the whole vault — documented as a one-time cost in user-facing release notes (initial re-embed of a large vault may take 30+ minutes against a real TEI sidecar; this is acceptable)

Obsidian-callout fixture test: `> [!note]\n> body line 1\n> body line 2` survives chunking as one cohesive chunk (CommonMark treats callouts as blockquotes; text-splitter should preserve blockquote blocks — verify and document the result)

Math-block fixture test: `$$\n\frac{a}{b} = c\n$$` is not split mid-equation. If text-splitter cuts mid-block (math blocks are not standard CommonMark), augment by wrapping math blocks in an HTML-block sentinel (e.g.
...
) before splitting and unwrapping after, OR pre-tokenizing math blocks as opaque atoms. Pick whichever keeps the implementation small.
Fenced code block fixture test: triple-backtick blocks (with and without language tags) are not split mid-fence

Frontmatter integration test (extends fixture_vault.rs from US-013): re-sync after redesign produces chunks with no YAML in chunk.text or embedding_text; meta --where on frontmatter still works; assert chunk.text for the first chunk of Filters/Frontmatter.md does not contain 'status:' or 'archived'
event_log writes continue to fire per-index call (the existing INSERT INTO event_log row in wiring.rs must not be removed by the refactor); existing change-tracking integration tests must still pass
US-022 (golden-set eval) will use post-US-013b chunker output as its calibration baseline — no action required in this story; this is a forward note for the loop, not a blocking dependency
just verify passes (the gate is `just verify`, which runs fmt + cargo check + clippy -D warnings + cargo test; never substitute `just check` which skips tests)
userStories.notes: Implemented text-splitter/tokenx-rs markdown chunking with body-only indexing, Obsidian comment stripping, token-aware chunk settings under [indexer], frontmatter-aware link resolution, config validation, release-note documentation, and regression coverage for parser fidelity, callouts, math/code blocks, trivial chunk filtering, and frontmatter exclusion. just verify passes.

userStories.title: Add MCP JSON-RPC stdio transport
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want `talon --mcp` to speak JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio so it can be embedded in MCP-aware agents.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-cli/src/mcp/{mod,transport,protocol}.rs
Choose between rmcp crate and hand-rolled JSON-RPC; document choice with reason in module docstring
Frame format: line-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 on stdin/stdout (no Content-Length headers)
Methods supported: initialize, initialized notification, tools/list, tools/call, shutdown
Tests: handshake echo round-trips; malformed frame returns proper JSON-RPC error envelope
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-cli/src/mcp/{mod,protocol,transport}.rs with documented hand-rolled JSON-RPC choice. Transport uses line-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 over generic BufRead/Write, supports initialize, initialized notification, tools/list, tools/call placeholder, and shutdown. Tests cover handshake round-trip and malformed-frame parse error envelope. just verify passes.

userStories.title: Add MCP tool schema and handler dispatch
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want a single `talon` tool whose action-discriminated input dispatches to the same query handlers the CLI uses.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-cli/src/mcp/tool.rs
Tool name: talon (per design spec §11)
Action union per spec §11 / Decision 10: search, read, sync, related, status, meta, changes, lint — and ONLY these (no embed action)
Input schema generated for each TalonInput variant (schemars crate or hand-written constants in tool.rs)
tools/call dispatches through the same handlers as US-006 through US-012; wraps responses in the US-005b envelope as MCP CallToolResult
TalonError mapped to MCP error envelope with code + message
Tests: tools/list returns valid schema; each action's tools/call round-trips against the integration vault
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-cli/src/mcp/tool.rs with the single talon tool, hand-written action schema for search/read/sync/status/related/meta/changes/lint only, and tools/call dispatch to the same core query/sync functions used by CLI handlers. MCP CallToolResult wraps full US-005b TalonEnvelope JSON in content text and structuredContent; invalid tool calls/arguments return an error envelope. Wired protocol.rs tools/list/tools/call to the module and enabled --mcp stdio loop in command.rs. Tests cover schema, invalid calls, status tools/call round-trip, and transport tools/list count. just verify passes.

userStories.title: Wire --mcp flag to stateless stdio request/response loop
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want `talon --mcp` to read JSON-RPC frames from stdin and write responses to stdout until EOF or shutdown — stateless per call (open DB, answer, return), no clock-driven work, per spec §4.
acceptanceCriteria: Replaces bail!("mcp mode is scaffolded but not implemented yet") in command.rs
Frame loop reads stdin until EOF or JSON-RPC shutdown method; SIGINT triggers clean exit
Each tools/call dispatches to the same handler the CLI uses, opens DB if needed, returns response — no in-memory caching beyond the existing SearchCache LRU
No background tasks, no timers, no fs watching inside the loop (spec §2 Decision 1)
Integration test: spawn `talon --mcp` subprocess, send initialize + tools/list + tools/call + shutdown, verify responses and clean exit
Manual smoke: `talon --mcp <<< '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{}}'` returns valid response
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Wired talon --mcp through command::run to the line-delimited JSON-RPC stdio loop. Existing transport handles EOF, initialize, initialized notification, tools/list, tools/call, and shutdown without background tasks. Added crates/talon-cli/tests/mcp_stdio.rs subprocess integration test covering initialize + tools/list + status tools/call + shutdown. Manual talon --mcp stdin smoke passes. just verify passes.

userStories.title: Scaffold ts/ npm package @seanmozeik/talon with binary resolver
userStories.description: As a Node consumer, I want a `@seanmozeik/talon` npm package (per Decision 14) that resolves the right prebuilt binary at runtime via optionalDependencies, matching the esbuild/biome/oxc/ddg pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: New ts/ workspace with package.json name `@seanmozeik/talon` declaring optionalDependencies: @seanmozeik/talon-darwin-arm64, @seanmozeik/talon-darwin-x64, @seanmozeik/talon-linux-x64, @seanmozeik/talon-linux-arm64, @seanmozeik/talon-win32-x64
ts/src/binary.ts: resolveBinary() walks optionalDependencies matching process.platform + process.arch, returns the binary path via require.resolve('@seanmozeik/talon-<plat>-<arch>/bin/talon')
ts/src/child.ts: mcpChildSpec(): { command, args: ['--mcp'], env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv } per design spec §9
ts/src/index.ts barrel exports mcpChildSpec and resolveBinary (the only public API per spec §9 — no Talon class, no .search()/.sync() methods)
Vitest tests for resolution logic with mocked platform/arch
npm pack produces a valid tarball; npm test clean
userStories.notes: Implemented ts npm package scaffold for @seanmozeik/talon: optional platform dependencies, resolveBinary() platform/arch resolver via require.resolve, mcpChildSpec() public API, Vitest coverage for resolution and child spec, package-lock, npm test, npm pack, and just verify pass.

userStories.title: Cross-build per-platform binaries with cargo zigbuild
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want `cargo zigbuild` to produce stripped release binaries for darwin-arm64, darwin-x64, linux-x64, linux-aarch64, win32-x64 so each @seanmozeik/talon-<platform>-<arch> npm subpackage ships a working binary.
acceptanceCriteria: Document zig + cargo-zigbuild install in DEVELOPMENT.md (or equivalent)

Build matrix script (justfile or shell) runs: cargo zigbuild --release --target aarch64-apple-darwin / x86_64-apple-darwin / x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu / aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu / x86_64-pc-windows-gnu
Each target output is stripped and < 30 MB
Each binary runs --version on its target platform (CI matrix or qemu/wine smoke)
Per-platform npm subpackage layout: package.json (name `@seanmozeik/talon-<platform>-<arch>`) + the prebuilt binary at bin/talon, no JS
just verify passes

userStories.title: Add release workflow and version tagging
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want a tag push to trigger the cross-build matrix and publish all platform packages plus the @seanmozeik/talon meta package to npm in one shot.
acceptanceCriteria: New .github/workflows/release.yml triggered by v* tag push
Matrix invokes US-018 build script, packages each platform binary, npm publish each @seanmozeik/talon-<platform>-<arch> subpackage and the @seanmozeik/talon meta package
Version is read from Cargo.toml; ts/package.json version stays in lockstep
Dry-run mode (workflow_dispatch with input) goes through every step except publish
Dry-run succeeds end-to-end on a clean checkout

userStories.title: Embed skill/SKILL.md and wire --skill flag
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want `talon --skill` to print an embedded SKILL.md (located at repo root `skill/SKILL.md` per spec §3 + Decision 12) so agents can self-load Talon usage instructions, matching the ddg pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Author skill/SKILL.md at repo root (port the agent prompt from the TS reference, then extend to document the new meta/changes/lint actions per spec §12)
include_str! embeds skill/SKILL.md into the talon-cli binary (path: include_str!("../../../skill/SKILL.md") from talon-cli/src/main.rs)
--skill prints the contents and exits 0
Test: assert_cmd verifies non-empty output + exit 0
just verify passes

userStories.title: Polish human CLI output
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want terminal-width-aware wrapping, colored headings, compact result cards, and a spinner during search so the human output is pleasant.
acceptanceCriteria: Add terminal_size dep (full semver) if not present
Search results render as compact cards: path, score, snippet — wrapped to terminal width
Headings use existing anstyle/anstream styling
Spinner from existing spinner module wired for search command
Snapshot tests via insta (one snapshot per command's human output)
just verify passes

userStories.title: Add ranking quality regression suite (golden-set eval with absolute nDCG floor)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a golden-set evaluation suite with absolute nDCG@5/MRR/Hit@k/Recall@k metrics so silent ranking quality drift is caught in CI — meeting and exceeding spec §19 Phase 1, and giving Talon a quality floor that survives the eventual TS reference retirement. Adopts the eval-system pattern from /home/yolo/.opensrc/repos/github.com/flowing-abyss/obsidian-hybrid-search/master/eval/ (golden-set JSON of {query, expected_paths[]}, ~100-line metrics module for nDCG/MRR/Hit@k/Recall@k, runner + A/B compare tool). Attribution: README.md and inline comments credit obsidian-hybrid-search (MIT licensed) for the eval harness shape.
acceptanceCriteria: Port tests/services/talon/ranking-regression.test.ts to crates/talon-core/tests/ranking_regression.rs (TS-parity bar: top-K paths match TS reference within tolerance — kept as a sub-assertion, not the only floor)

Eval harness at crates/talon-core/tests/eval/: golden-set JSON (fixtures/golden-set.json) with 30-60 {query, expected_paths[]} pairs derived from the US-013 fixture vault; metrics module computes nDCG@5, nDCG@10, MRR, Hit@5, Hit@10, Recall@10 (port eval/metrics.ts logic, attribution in module docstring)
Eval runner (tests/eval_suite.rs) executes golden set against freshly-synced fixture vault and writes JSON results to tests/eval/results/ (gitignored except baseline.json)

Commit baseline file tests/eval/baseline.json (canonical reference); regression test asserts measured metrics never fall below FLOOR thresholds (e.g. nDCG@5 >= 0.65 — calibrated post-US-013b, raised never lowered)

Compare tool (justfile recipe `just eval-compare <a.json> <b.json>` or tests/eval/compare.rs) reports per-query nDCG delta between two runs — used during iteration on chunker / expansion / rerank changes
Documentation (DEVELOPMENT.md or tests/eval/README.md): how to run eval, how to update baseline (only when metrics genuinely improve), thresholds are raised never lowered
FLOOR thresholds calibrated against post-US-013b chunker output (this story lands after US-013b)
Score drift on data.results[*].score tolerated within 1e-3; rank order changes fail the parity sub-assertion
Stable subset of envelope meta fields asserted (result_count, scope set, fast flag) — not the full meta blob
Any TS-vs-Rust intentional divergence documented inline with reason
just verify passes

userStories.title: Rich match anchors API: previewAnchors with char offsets, DOM-matchable text, heading breadcrumb
userStories.description: As a downstream Talon consumer (ultraclaw, future Obsidian plugin, agents), I want each search result to optionally carry up to two MatchAnchor entries (BM25 anchor + semantic anchor, deduped by match_text) plus a heading breadcrumb prepended to every snippet, so I can scroll-to and highlight the exact matching block in the rendered note — not just the matching note. Pattern lifted from /home/yolo/.opensrc/repos/github.com/flowing-abyss/obsidian-hybrid-search/master/src/searcher.ts (MatchAnchor type :40-46, heading-breadcrumb-prepended snippets :265-273, two-strategy heading lookup with content-scan fallback :460-515) and src/chunker.ts (buildMatchText DOM-matchable text builder :164-199). Attribution: README.md and module docstrings credit obsidian-hybrid-search (MIT licensed).
acceptanceCriteria: New type MatchAnchor in talon-core/src/tool.rs: { kind: AnchorKind (Bm25 | Semantic), heading_path: Option<String>, match_text: String, char_start: Option<u32>, char_end: Option<u32> } — serialized in camelCase per existing envelope conventions
SearchResult gains preview_anchors: Option<Vec<MatchAnchor>>, populated only when SearchInput.anchors == Some(true) — opt-in to keep default response payload small

BM25 snippet path with anchors=true: attach Bm25 anchor with heading_path resolved via chunks.heading_path (Strategy 1: chunk lookup); fall back to content-scan reconstruction (Strategy 2: locate snippet text in notes.content, walk preceding lines to rebuild heading chain). Port searcher.ts:469-515 logic.
Semantic snippet path with anchors=true: attach Semantic anchor with the chunk's heading_path, char_start/char_end, and match_text built from chunk text

buildMatchText port (talon-core/src/search/match_text.rs): strips heading lines, fenced code, callout-only lines, HTML tags, footnote refs (`[^1]`), embed wikilinks (`![[...]]`), image embeds, regular wikilinks (resolve to alias|target), inline links (keep text), bold/italic, inline code, list markers, task checkboxes; returns first 80 chars of body — matches what textContent returns from a rendered Obsidian DOM block

Anchor dedup: when both BM25 and semantic anchors yield equal match_text (same block), keep only the BM25 anchor (heuristic: BM25 is positionally precise; matches reference behavior at searcher.ts:1227)

Heading breadcrumb prepended to snippets unconditionally (independent of anchors flag) — port searcher.ts:265-273; format `{heading_path}\n{snippet}`. Search snippet rendering in CLI human output (output.rs::emit_search_human) shows the breadcrumb
MCP tool schema (US-015): SearchInput gains anchors: Option<bool>; output schema documents preview_anchors as optional

Unit tests: buildMatchText against fixture chunk texts (covers all syntax-stripping paths); anchors=true returns up to 2 anchors per result; dedup logic suppresses duplicate when chunks coincide; content-scan fallback works for whole-note chunks (no chunk-level heading_path granular enough)
Integration test: search with --anchors against fixture vault returns preview_anchors with char_start/char_end correctly indexing into source file body (post-US-013b body-relative offsets)
just verify passes

userStories.title: Add CLI contract test suite
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want assert_cmd-driven contract tests so the CLI's exit codes, help text, and JSON envelope shape are pinned and cannot regress silently.
acceptanceCriteria: New crates/talon-cli/tests/cli.rs using assert_cmd + predicates
Covers: help text shape, exit code per error class
JSON envelope shape per Decision 8: top-level keys are exactly {action, version, ok, data, meta} on success and {action, version, ok, error} on failure — verified for every command
Ports relevant assertions from tests/services/talon/cli.test.ts and contract.test.ts
Suite green; just verify passes

userStories.title: Verification sweep: NFD paths, trigram FTS, vector norm, WAL, RRF normalization, per-note dedup, rerank tiers
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want a single sweep that verifies (and fixes if needed) a set of correctness/perf/relevance details that obsidian-hybrid-search gets right and that easily slip in a Rust port — before tagging v0.1.0. Most items are likely already correct; the goal is explicit verification with regression tests, not blanket re-implementation. References: /home/yolo/.opensrc/repos/github.com/flowing-abyss/obsidian-hybrid-search/master/src/db.ts and searcher.ts (MIT licensed; attribution in inline comments where logic is ported). Each item lists the verifier's evidence path.
acceptanceCriteria: NFD path normalization: all paths in notes.path / links.from_path / links.to_path / scope filters are NFD-normalized via unicode-normalization crate (or std equivalent). Test: NFC and NFD forms of the same Unicode filename store under the same row. Reference: db.ts:443.

Trigram FTS for fuzzy title/alias: confirm Talon's search_title_parts uses SQLite's trigram tokenizer (tokenize='trigram'). Test: 'Cafe' matches 'Café'; Cyrillic substring search works without external collation. If not, switch and add migration. Reference: db.ts:368-411.

Short-alias exact-match fallback: aliases < 3 chars can't be trigram-tokenized. Confirm an alias_norm column (NFD + lowercased) supports a parallel exact-match path that runs first and dedupes against trigram results. Test: fixture note with 2-char alias is findable. Reference: searcher.ts:336-369.

Vector unit-norm: assert ||v|| ≈ 1.0 (within 1e-4) for sampled vec_chunks rows. If sidecar doesn't normalize, normalize at persist time (embed/persist.rs). Without unit norm, the cosine-from-L2 identity fails.
Cosine from L2 conversion: semantic search converts sqlite-vec L2 distance to cosine via similarity = max(0, 1 - distance² / 2) before scoring. Reference: searcher.ts:691. If using raw L2, switch.
WAL journal mode: rusqlite Connection sets PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL on open (so --mcp can read while talon sync writes). Reference: db.ts:472.

Per-note vector dedup: semantic-search SQL uses ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY note_id ORDER BY distance, chunk_index) WHERE row_num = 1 — one chunk per note in the candidate pool. Reference: searcher.ts:655-672. Test: a note with 5 overlapping near-duplicate chunks contributes a single result, not five.
Vector candidate pool sizing: semantic search retrieves k = limit * 5 candidates from sqlite-vec before per-note dedup. Document rationale inline (recall depends on it). Reference: searcher.ts:674.

RRF score normalization to [0,1]: fuse::reciprocal_rank_fusion divides final scores by max_possible = Σweights / (k+1). Test: hybrid score for the top result ≤ 1.0 within float epsilon. Reference: searcher.ts:748-759.

Position-aware rerank tier blend with min-max normalization: rerank_pipeline applies tiers (ranks 0-9: 0.75 hybrid + 0.25 rerank, 10-19: 0.6/0.4, 20+: 0.4/0.6) AND min-max normalizes hybrid scores within the candidate batch before blending with sigmoid(logit). Reference: searcher.ts:1299-1325. Without min-max, raw hybrid scores wash out the rerank contribution. Add a unit test that confirms the normalization step.

Cache key includes db_version + model_version: SearchCache key invalidates on schema migration AND embed-model change. Add db_version row to settings table if absent (cross-process invalidation). Reference: searcher.ts:982 + db.ts:249-250.

Prepared statement caching (do better than reference): hot-path SQL in upsert.rs / search modules uses Connection::prepare_cached, not Connection::prepare each call. Survey + switch where applicable. The reference re-prepares on every call (db.ts is full of `db.prepare(...)` inside functions); we deliberately don't.
Each item adds a regression test or cites the existing test that verifies it; no item marked done without explicit evidence in the user story notes field
just verify passes

userStories.title: Final verification: release build, parity, README, manual smokes
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want every shippable surface verified: release build, parity against TS for canonical queries, README explains the inference endpoint, manual smoke of every command — before tagging v0.1.0.
acceptanceCriteria: just verify green
cargo build --release succeeds; stripped binary < 30 MB
talon --version, talon --help, talon --skill all work
talon init creates ~/.config/talon/config.toml from template

talon sync indexes a real vault end-to-end AND embeds pending chunks against a live TEI sidecar in the same call (per US-004b spec-faithful sync; the caller — ultraclaw's chokidar watcher or a launchd plist — is responsible for invoking sync; Rust does not watch)
talon sync --fast completes without contacting the inference sidecar (lexical pass only)
talon search '<query>' returns real ranked results with --where and --since filtering working (manual smoke)
talon related <path>, talon meta --tag-counts, talon lint orphans all work on real data
talon --mcp survives initialize + tools/list + tools/call + shutdown round-trip from an MCP inspector or hand-crafted JSON-RPC; tools/list does NOT advertise an embed action (Decision 10)
README explains inference.base_url points at a TEI-compatible endpoint (text-embeddings-inference, Infinity, or ultraclaw's sidecar) per Decision 13
JSON output for every command matches the {action, version, ok, data, meta} envelope shape (Decision 8) verified by US-023 contract tests

Parity bar §19 Phase 1: 5+ canonical search queries return matching data.results[*].path against TS reference (top-K order must match; data.results[*].score drift within 1e-3 allowed; stable subset of meta fields equal)
v0.1.0 tag pushed; US-019 release workflow publishes successfully (dry-run first if uncertain)

userStories.title: talon recall: vault-native context recall command for agent lifecycle hooks
userStories.description: As an agent host's lifecycle hook (Hermes Memory Provider, hand-rolled wrappers), I want a single command `talon recall` that takes the user's recent message + a token budget + an exclude-paths list, and returns a structured Obsidian-native recall payload (active_notes, linked_context, frontmatter, recent_edits, fuzzy_anchors) plus a calibrated evidence_score, so I can splice high-quality vault context into the agent's prompt without a tool round-trip and without injecting low-confidence noise. Stateless per spec — caller owns turn state and exclusion lists. Talon's job is recall ONLY; retain (writing to vault) and reflect (synthesizing/pruning) are the agent host's job, performed via the user's existing editor or a separate cron-driven agent. We assume the Obsidian vault is bulletproof and authoritative — our job is to surface the most relevant context from it. Section schema is grounded in Talon's actual data surfaces, not imported biomimetic taxonomy: every section maps 1:1 to an existing query module. Scoring formulas are calibrated composites motivated by published patterns (MemoryBank Ebbinghaus decay, Mem0/Zep dual-signal composites, Confidence Gate pattern from RAG literature). Depends on US-013b (chunker redesign + tokenx-rs dep) being complete — token budget math reuses the same tokenx-rs estimator.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/query/recall.rs with run_recall(conn, inference, expansion, &RecallInput) -> RecallResponse

New CLI verb `talon recall <message...>` with flags: --prior-message <text>... (repeatable, last N user/assistant turns), --budget-tokens <N> (default 2000), --exclude <vault-path>... (repeatable), --scope <scope>... (repeatable; reuses ScopePriority), --since <ts> (default: 7 days ago), --format json|prompt-xml (default json), --depth <N> (default 1, max 3), --recency-half-life-days <N> (default 7), --min-confidence <0.0-1.0> (default 0.0; below this, return empty payload — see confidence-gate AC below), --fast
MCP action `recall` with RecallInput / RecallResponse added to TalonInput / TalonResponseData; tool surface advertises it in tools/list (US-015)

Internal pipeline reuses existing modules: hybrid_pipeline (active_notes), query::related at depth N with direction=Both (linked_context — both outgoing AND backlinks; differs from US-008's default of Outgoing because recall wants 360° context), query::meta + note_frontmatter_fields (frontmatter), query::changes since cutoff (recent_edits), search_title_parts + alias matching below the main score threshold (fuzzy_anchors); NO new retrieval algorithms

--prior-message behavior pinned: prior messages are concatenated with the user message and fed to the expansion LLM (when expansion is enabled / --fast is absent) to widen the implicit query. Prior messages are NOT fed directly to the search query — would dilute the BM25 signal. When --fast is set, prior messages are dropped entirely (latency-critical path skips expansion).

RecallResponse data shape: { vault_recall: { active_notes: Vec<NoteExcerpt>, linked_context: Vec<LinkedNote>, frontmatter: Vec<FrontmatterFact>, recent_edits: Vec<EditedNote>, fuzzy_anchors: Vec<FuzzyAnchor> } | null, evidence_score: f64, tokens_used: u32, excluded: Vec<String>, excluded_by_budget: Vec<String>, skipped: bool }. excluded carries paths suppressed via --exclude (caller's list); excluded_by_budget carries paths retrieved-but-not-included due to budget pressure (the inverse — caller can know what almost made it in).

evidence_score formula (pinned in talon-core/src/query/recall_scoring.rs): 0.45 * top_rerank_score + 0.20 * top_lexical_indicator + 0.15 * graph_density_bonus + 0.10 * recency_bonus + 0.10 * frontmatter_match_indicator. graph_density_bonus = min(1.0, top_result_link_count / 5). recency_bonus = exp(-days_since_top_result_modified / 14). All terms in [0,1]; result clamped to [0,1].

evidence_score edge cases pinned: when --fast skips rerank, top_rerank_score is substituted with the top hybrid_pipeline score (already in [0,1] since US-023a normalized RRF). When zero results return, evidence_score = 0.0 and skipped is automatically true (no formula evaluation).

evidence_score weights are v1 calibration — re-tuned later via US-022 eval suite. Document this in the recall_scoring.rs module docstring with attribution to MemoryBank/Mem0/Zep/Cognee literature; weights are NOT a config knob in v1.

Golden test fixture for recall_scoring.rs: at least 5 synthetic input triplets (top_rerank_score, lexical_hit, link_count, days_since_modified, frontmatter_hit) with their expected evidence_score values to within 1e-4. Include cases for: zero-result (expect 0.0), rerank-skipped (substitute hybrid score), all-signals-strong (expect ≥0.9), only-semantic-strong (expect ~0.45), only-frontmatter-match (expect ~0.10).

recent_edits ordering: recent_edits_score = 0.6 * topic_relevance + 0.4 * exp(-days_since_modified / recency_half_life_days). Within the --since window, results sorted by this composite, NOT chronologically. Items outside the window are dropped entirely (--since is the hard cutoff; the half-life is the weighting inside it).

Confidence gate behavior: when evidence_score < --min-confidence, response is { vault_recall: null, evidence_score, tokens_used: 0, excluded: [], excluded_by_budget: [], skipped: true }. In --format prompt-xml, skipped output is a single self-closing tag: <vault_recall skipped="true" evidence_score="0.18"/> — NO <active_notes> etc. children. Plugin layer can drop this entirely or pass it through; agent never sees polluted context either way.

Token budget enforced greedily across sections; section priority order: active_notes → linked_context → frontmatter → recent_edits → fuzzy_anchors. Per-section ranking keys for trimming: active_notes / linked_context use hybrid_score (post-RRF, post-rerank); frontmatter facts use a flat 0.5 (frontmatter is binary present/absent — preserved or dropped wholesale per matched note, not per fact); recent_edits use recent_edits_score; fuzzy_anchors use match_score. Trimming algorithm: while tokens_used > budget_tokens, drop the lowest-ranked item from the lowest-priority not-yet-empty section, recompute tokens_used, repeat. Items dropped during budget trimming go into excluded_by_budget.
--exclude applied to retrieval candidates BEFORE budget allocation — never spend budget on excluded paths
Token counting uses tokenx-rs (the same crate from US-013b) for the token cap math; full envelope target tokens_used <= budget_tokens within ±2% slack to account for estimator imprecision

--format prompt-xml renderer in talon-cli/src/output.rs emits a <vault_recall source="talon" vault="..." evidence_score="..."> XML block with <active_notes>, <linked_context>, <frontmatter>, <recent_edits>, <fuzzy_anchors> children. Each child element shape is documented in docs/recall-schema.md with attribute and content rules; an example committed at examples/recall-output.xml. The doc and example are deliverables of this story, not future work.

Wraps response in the US-005b envelope ({action: "recall", version, ok, data, meta} on success; standard error envelope on failure). meta carries elapsed_ms, fast flag, scope set, result_count, evidence_score (duplicated from data for diagnostics when data is null due to confidence gate).

Integration test (extends fixture_vault.rs from US-013): full pipeline against fixture vault with mocked sidecar. Asserts: all five sections populate when applicable; --exclude suppresses paths and they appear in `excluded`; --budget-tokens 200 produces a proportionally smaller payload (no overshoot >2%) AND populates excluded_by_budget; --min-confidence 0.99 returns skipped=true for queries with no strong matches; --depth 0 produces empty linked_context; --fast skips expansion (verified by absence of expansion call to mocked sidecar); prompt-xml output validates against the documented schema.

Documentation: docs/recall.md explains the section semantics, the scoring formulas (with the literature attribution), and the integration patterns for Hermes / hand-rolled hooks. References docs/recall-schema.md for the prompt-xml shape.

NO latency benchmarks in Ralph-loop ACs: latency optimization is deferred — the LLM is the bottleneck per design, and benchmarks in CI cause more flakes than they prevent. A future story can add latency regression tests once usage patterns are clearer.
just verify passes

userStories.title: Hermes Memory Provider plugin: hermes-talon-recall (light wrapper, unit-tested only)
userStories.description: As a Hermes Agent user with an Obsidian vault, I want a Memory Provider plugin that wraps `talon recall --format prompt-xml` so my Hermes agent gets vault-native context recall on every turn without me writing glue code. The plugin is intentionally a thin shim — Talon's binary does the work; the plugin matches Hermes's documented Memory Provider plugin layout (plugin.yaml + Python module subclassing the abstract base in `agent/memory_provider.py` of the Hermes repo) and shells out to the talon binary discovered on PATH. Layout reference: study `plugins/memory/supermemory/` and `plugins/memory/honcho/` in the NousResearch/hermes-agent repo as canonical examples (in-tree built-in plugins use the same surface as third-party plugins). Hermes plugin docs: hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/developer-guide/memory-provider-plugin/. Hermes plugin v0.11.x surface (April 23 2026): plugins drop into ~/.hermes/plugins/<name>/ with plugin.yaml manifest, register(ctx) callback wires hooks/tools/CLI commands. Empty-output behavior: when talon returns skipped=true or zero results, the plugin returns Hermes's no-recall envelope so the agent sees a clean context — never injects polluted output. Real end-to-end integration testing against a live Hermes install is OUT OF SCOPE for the Ralph loop (causes flakes; will be done manually post-loop). Ralph delivers: correct layout, manifest, subprocess wrapper, unit tests with mocked binary.
acceptanceCriteria: New directory integrations/hermes-talon-recall/ in this repo (single-repo for v0.1; split to a separate repo later if release cadence diverges)

Layout matches Hermes plugin convention discovered from the canonical built-in plugins (study plugins/memory/supermemory/ in the NousResearch/hermes-agent repo before locking the layout): pyproject.toml, plugin.yaml, hermes_talon_recall/__init__.py, hermes_talon_recall/provider.py, tests/test_provider.py, README.md

plugin.yaml manifest at minimum: name=talon-recall, version=<lockstep-with-talon-cli>, provides_hooks (or provides_memory_provider — confirm the actual key by reading the Hermes plugin docs and a working plugin), description, author. The manifest schema is whatever the current Hermes v0.11.x plugin loader expects — match it exactly, do not invent fields.

Provider class subclasses the abstract Memory Provider base in agent/memory_provider.py of the Hermes repo. Implement all abstract methods the base class declares (recall, search, ingest if applicable, etc.). For methods Talon doesn't service (ingest / retain / reflect — Talon is recall-only), return Hermes's documented no-op envelope or raise the documented NotSupportedError, whichever the base class specifies. Read the base class to know which.

Binary discovery: shutil.which("talon") on PATH. Fallback: TALON_BIN env var pointing at an absolute binary path. If neither resolves, raise a clear error during plugin init (NOT at recall-time) with a message pointing to Talon install instructions.

recall() implementation: shells out to `talon recall --format prompt-xml --budget-tokens [--prior-message ...] [--exclude

...] [--min-confidence ] [--fast if configured]`. Captures stdout, returns the XML directly to Hermes (no re-parsing, no re-formatting).

Empty-output handling: when the talon process returns skipped=true (detected via the <vault_recall skipped="true" .../> self-closing tag), the plugin returns Hermes's no-recall envelope so the agent context stays clean. Same for zero-byte stdout or non-zero exit. Agent NEVER sees a malformed XML block, an error stack trace, or polluted context — failures are silent from the agent's perspective and logged to the plugin's logger only.

Configurable from Hermes plugin config (per Hermes config conventions): vault_path (passed to talon via --vault flag or TALON_VAULT env var), budget_tokens (default 2000), min_confidence (default 0.3), recency_half_life_days (default 7), fast (default false), prior_message_count (how many of the last N turns to feed via --prior-message; default 2)

Unit tests in tests/test_provider.py: mock subprocess.run to return canned talon stdout (good case, skipped case, error case, empty stdout, malformed XML). Assert the plugin's recall() method returns the right Hermes envelope shape in each case. NO live Hermes process required; NO live talon binary required.

README.md in integrations/hermes-talon-recall/: install instructions (drop directory into ~/.hermes/plugins/talon-recall/, OR pip install -e . for dev, OR pip install hermes-talon-recall once published), configuration example showing all knobs, troubleshooting section (binary not found → install talon and check PATH; vault path missing → set vault_path or TALON_VAULT; agent gets no context → check min_confidence threshold; agent gets stale context → reduce recency_half_life_days)

PyPI package metadata in pyproject.toml: name=hermes-talon-recall, license=MIT (matches Talon), description, author/contact, homepage = Talon repo URL. Version stays lockstep with talon-cli major.minor (verified by a script or pyproject.toml comment). Python >= 3.11 (matches Hermes's stated minimum)
Talon main README gains an `## Integrations` section linking to integrations/hermes-talon-recall/README.md; plugin's README links back to the talon recall reference docs from US-025

NO end-to-end integration test against a real Hermes install in the Ralph loop. The plugin's correctness is verified at the unit level only. Real Hermes integration testing is a manual step the user performs post-loop with `hermes plugins install <path>` and a vault.
NO latency benchmarks. LLM round-trip dominates; talon binary startup is sub-50ms in Rust release builds and is not the bottleneck.

A `release-plugin.yml` workflow OR a justfile recipe (whichever is simpler) that builds the Python package via `python -m build` and optionally publishes to PyPI on a `hermes-plugin-v*` tag — separate from the main Talon release tag so plugin releases don't fire on every Talon release

Verification deliverable in user story notes upon completion: paste the contents of plugin.yaml, the abstract method signatures the provider implements, and the result of `python -m pytest tests/` showing all unit tests pass. This makes the manual integration test post-loop a 5-minute exercise instead of a debugging session.
